1	"""Per-variant runner for variants A (scope-aware ReAct), B (Plan-and-Solve v2), C (mismatched).
2	
3	Tool registries: Tau-bench and FrontierScience are reasoning-bound and use a stub registry with a
4	single ``python_exec`` tool that runs Python in a short-timeout subprocess; SWE-bench in this task
5	is also driven by reasoning only — the harness does NOT execute SWE-bench patches inside isolated
6	environments. The judge module documents this scope constraint.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10

13	from collections.abc import Callable

59	@dataclass(slots=True)
60	class VariantRunSummary:
61	    variant: str
62	    n_instances: int
63	    predictions: list[Prediction] = field(default_factory=list)
64	    elapsed_seconds: float = 0.0
65	
66	
67	def _synthetic_annotation(*, problem_text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
68	    snippet = problem_text.strip()[:500]
69	    if len(snippet) == 0:
70	        snippet = "(empty problem)"
71	    return {
72	        "hierarchy": {
73	            "global": f"Solve the following problem end-to-end: {snippet}",
74	            "subtasks": [
75	                {
76	                    "subtask": "Restate the problem and identify the target output type.",
77	                    "atomics": [
78	                        "Read the problem carefully.",
79	                        "List any quantities or constraints mentioned in the problem.",
80	                    ],
81	                },
82	                {
83	                    "subtask": "Plan the solution and produce a final answer.",
84	                    "atomics": [
85	                        "Outline the solution steps.",
86	                        "Compute or formulate the final answer.",
87	                    ],
88	                },
89	            ],
90	            "global_atomics": [
91	                "Emit the final answer with a Finish action.",
92	            ],
93	        }
94	    }
95	
96	
97	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
98	class _InstanceOutcome:
99	    instance_id: str
100	    subset: str
101	    final_answer: str | None
102	    final_confidence: float | None
103	    cost_usd: float
104	    trajectory_payload: Any
105	
106	
107	def _process_one_instance(
108	    *,
109	    variant: VariantId,
110	    instance: Instance,
111	    cost_tracker: CostTracker,
112	    model_id: str,
113	    variant_dir: Path,
114	    max_turns: int,
115	    max_tokens: int,
116	) -> _InstanceOutcome:
117	    local_tracker = CostTracker()
118	    model_call = make_model_call(
119	        model_id=model_id,
120	        cost_tracker=cost_tracker,
121	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
122	        secondary_cost_tracker=local_tracker,
123	    )
124	    traj_path = variant_dir / f"trajectory_{instance.instance_id}.json"
125	    final_answer: str | None = None
126	    final_confidence: float | None = None
127	    trajectory_payload: Any
128	    try:
129	        if variant == VARIANT_A:
130	            react_result = _run_variant_a(
131	                instance=instance,
132	                model_call=model_call,
133	                trajectory_path=traj_path.with_suffix(".jsonl"),
134	                max_turns=max_turns,
135	            )
136	            final_answer = react_result.answer
137	            trajectory_payload = _trajectory_to_jsonable(obj=react_result.trajectory)
138	        elif variant == VARIANT_B:
139	            v2_result = _run_variant_b(
140	                instance=instance,
141	                model_call=model_call,
142	                max_turns=max_turns,
143	            )
144	            final_answer = v2_result.final_answer
145	            final_confidence = v2_result.final_confidence
146	            trajectory_payload = _trajectory_to_jsonable(obj=v2_result.trajectory)
147	        elif variant == VARIANT_C:
148	            mismatch_result = _run_variant_c(instance=instance, model_call=model_call)
149	            final_answer = mismatch_result.final_answer
150	            trajectory_payload = _trajectory_to_jsonable(obj=mismatch_result.trajectory)
151	        else:
152	            raise ValueError(f"unknown variant: {variant!r}")
153	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — surface as a recorded failure and continue
154	        trajectory_payload = {"error": f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc!s}"[:500]}
155	    cost_for_instance = float(local_tracker.snapshot()["cost_usd"])
156	    return _InstanceOutcome(
157	        instance_id=instance.instance_id,
158	        subset=instance.subset,
159	        final_answer=final_answer,
160	        final_confidence=final_confidence,
161	        cost_usd=cost_for_instance,
162	        trajectory_payload=trajectory_payload,
163	    )
164	
165	
166	def _run_variant_a(
167	    *,
168	    instance: Instance,
169	    model_call: Callable[[str], str],
170	    trajectory_path: Path,
171	    max_turns: int,
172	) -> ReactAgentResult:
173	    tool_registry = build_react_tool_registry()
174	    agent = ScopeAwareReactAgent(
175	        problem=instance.problem_text,
176	        granularity="atomic",
177	        tool_registry=tool_registry,
178	        model_call=model_call,
179	        trajectory_path=trajectory_path,
180	        max_turns=max_turns,
181	    )
182	    return agent.run()
183	
184	
185	def _run_variant_b(
186	    *,
187	    instance: Instance,
188	    model_call: Callable[[str], str],
189	    max_turns: int,
190	) -> PlanAndSolveV2Result:
191	    tool_registry = build_planandsolve_tool_registry()
192	    agent = PlanAndSolveAgentV2(
193	        model_call=model_call,
194	        tool_registry=dict(tool_registry),
195	        max_steps=max_turns,
196	    )
197	    return agent.run(problem=instance.problem_text)
198	
199	
200	def _run_variant_c(
201	    *,
202	    instance: Instance,
203	    model_call: Callable[[str], str],
204	) -> MismatchAgentResult:
205	    tool_registry = build_planandsolve_tool_registry()
206	    agent = MatchedMismatchAgent(
207	        model_call=model_call,
208	        tool_registry=tool_registry,
209	        delegate="scope_aware_react",
210	        mismatch_strategy="adversarial",
211	        seed=0,
212	    )
213	    annotation = _synthetic_annotation(problem_text=instance.problem_text)
214	    return agent.run(problem=instance.problem_text, annotation=annotation)
215	
216	
217	def _trajectory_to_jsonable(*, obj: Any) -> Any:
218	    if hasattr(obj, "__dataclass_fields__"):
219	        return asdict(obj)
220	    if isinstance(obj, list):
221	        return [_trajectory_to_jsonable(obj=x) for x in obj]
222	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
223	        return {k: _trajectory_to_jsonable(obj=v) for k, v in obj.items()}
224	    return obj
225	
226	
227	def run_variant(
228	    *,
229	    variant: VariantId,
230	    instances: list[Instance],
231	    cost_tracker: CostTracker,
232	    model_id: str,
233	    output_dir: Path,
234	    max_turns: int = _DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS,
235	    max_tokens: int = 4096,
236	    max_workers: int = _DEFAULT_MAX_WORKERS,
237	) -> VariantRunSummary:
238	    variant_dir = output_dir / variant
239	    variant_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
240	    summary = VariantRunSummary(variant=variant, n_instances=len(instances))
241	    started = time.monotonic()
242	    instance_by_id: dict[str, Instance] = {inst.instance_id: inst for inst in instances}
243	    outcomes: dict[str, _InstanceOutcome] = {}
244	    workers = max(1, min(max_workers, len(instances)))
245	    if workers <= 1 or len(instances) <= 1:
246	        for instance in instances:
247	            outcome = _process_one_instance(
248	                variant=variant,
249	                instance=instance,
250	                cost_tracker=cost_tracker,
251	                model_id=model_id,
252	                variant_dir=variant_dir,
253	                max_turns=max_turns,
254	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
255	            )
256	            outcomes[outcome.instance_id] = outcome
257	            _persist_outcome(
258	                outcome=outcome,
259	                variant=variant,
260	                variant_dir=variant_dir,
261	            )
262	    else:
263	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers) as executor:
264	            future_to_iid = {
265	                executor.submit(
266	                    _process_one_instance,
267	                    variant=variant,
268	                    instance=instance,
269	                    cost_tracker=cost_tracker,
270	                    model_id=model_id,
271	                    variant_dir=variant_dir,
272	                    max_turns=max_turns,
273	                    max_tokens=max_tokens,
274	                ): instance.instance_id
275	                for instance in instances
276	            }
277	            total = len(future_to_iid)
278	            for done, future in enumerate(as_completed(future_to_iid), start=1):
279	                iid = future_to_iid[future]
280	                try:
281	                    outcome = future.result()
282	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
283	                    inst = instance_by_id[iid]
284	                    outcome = _InstanceOutcome(
285	                        instance_id=iid,
286	                        subset=inst.subset,
287	                        final_answer=None,
288	                        final_confidence=None,
289	                        cost_usd=0.0,
290	                        trajectory_payload={
291	                            "error": f"executor: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc!s}"[:500]
292	                        },
293	                    )
294	                outcomes[outcome.instance_id] = outcome
295	                _persist_outcome(
296	                    outcome=outcome,
297	                    variant=variant,
298	                    variant_dir=variant_dir,
299	                )
300	                if done % 5 == 0 or done == total:
301	                    elapsed = time.monotonic() - started
302	                    print(f"    variant {variant}: {done}/{total} done, elapsed {elapsed:.1f}s")
303	    for instance in instances:
304	        outcome = outcomes[instance.instance_id]
305	        traj_path = variant_dir / f"trajectory_{instance.instance_id}.json"
306	        summary.predictions.append(
307	            Prediction(
308	                instance_id=outcome.instance_id,
309	                subset=outcome.subset,
310	                variant=variant,
311	                final_answer=outcome.final_answer,
312	                final_confidence=outcome.final_confidence,
313	                cost_usd=outcome.cost_usd,
314	                trajectory_path=str(traj_path),
315	            )
316	        )
317	    summary.elapsed_seconds = time.monotonic() - started
318	    return summary
319	
320	
321	def _persist_outcome(
322	    *,
323	    outcome: _InstanceOutcome,
324	    variant: VariantId,
325	    variant_dir: Path,
326	) -> None:
327	    traj_path = variant_dir / f"trajectory_{outcome.instance_id}.json"
328	    record = {
329	        "instance_id": outcome.instance_id,
330	        "subset": outcome.subset,
331	        "variant": variant,
332	        "final_answer": outcome.final_answer,
333	        "final_confidence": outcome.final_confidence,
334	        "cost_usd": outcome.cost_usd,
335	        "trajectory": outcome.trajectory_payload,
336	    }
337	    traj_path.write_text(json.dumps(record, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
338

1	"""Anthropic-backed model-call shim with cost tallying and retry-with-backoff."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	import os
7	import subprocess
8	import threading
9	import time
10	from collections.abc import Callable
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12	from typing import Any, Final
13	
14	from tasks.t0026_phase2_abc_runtime_n147_for_rq1_rq5.code.paths import (
15	    FORBIDDEN_HAIKU_MODEL_ID,
16	)
17	
18	_TRANSPORT_ENV_VAR: Final[str] = "T0026_TRANSPORT"
19	_TRANSPORT_CLI: Final[str] = "cli"
20	_TRANSPORT_SDK: Final[str] = "sdk"
21	_DEFAULT_TRANSPORT: Final[str] = _TRANSPORT_CLI
22	
23	_MAX_RETRIES: Final[int] = 2
24	_INITIAL_BACKOFF_SECONDS: Final[float] = 2.0
25	_MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS: Final[float] = 8.0
26	_CLI_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: Final[float] = 180.0
27	
28	SONNET_INPUT_PER_MTOK_USD: Final[float] = 3.0
29	SONNET_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK_USD: Final[float] = 15.0
30	OPUS_INPUT_PER_MTOK_USD: Final[float] = 15.0
31	OPUS_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK_USD: Final[float] = 75.0
32	
33	_PRICE_TABLE: Final[dict[str, tuple[float, float]]] = {
34	    "claude-sonnet-4-6": (SONNET_INPUT_PER_MTOK_USD, SONNET_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK_USD),
35	    "claude-opus-4-7": (OPUS_INPUT_PER_MTOK_USD, OPUS_OUTPUT_PER_MTOK_USD),
36	}
37	
38	
39	def _published_price(*, model_id: str, prompt_tokens: int, completion_tokens: int) -> float:
40	    if model_id not in _PRICE_TABLE:
41	        return 0.0
42	    in_rate, out_rate = _PRICE_TABLE[model_id]
43	    return prompt_tokens * in_rate / 1_000_000.0 + completion_tokens * out_rate / 1_000_000.0
44	
45	
46	@dataclass(slots=True)
47	class CostTracker:
48	    prompt_tokens: int = 0
49	    completion_tokens: int = 0
50	    cost_usd: float = 0.0
51	    n_calls: int = 0
52	    parse_failures: int = 0
53	    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock)
54	
55	    def record(self, *, model_id: str, prompt_tokens: int, completion_tokens: int) -> None:
56	        with self._lock:
57	            self.prompt_tokens += prompt_tokens
58	            self.completion_tokens += completion_tokens
59	            self.cost_usd += _published_price(
60	                model_id=model_id,
61	                prompt_tokens=prompt_tokens,
62	                completion_tokens=completion_tokens,
63	            )
64	            self.n_calls += 1
65	
66	    def note_parse_failure(self) -> None:
67	        with self._lock:
68	            self.parse_failures += 1
69	
70	    def snapshot(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
71	        with self._lock:
72	            return {
73	                "prompt_tokens": self.prompt_tokens,
74	                "completion_tokens": self.completion_tokens,
75	                "cost_usd": self.cost_usd,
76	                "n_calls": self.n_calls,
77	                "parse_failures": self.parse_failures,
78	            }
79	
80	
81	def _selected_transport() -> str:
82	    raw = os.environ.get(_TRANSPORT_ENV_VAR, _DEFAULT_TRANSPORT).strip().lower()
83	    if raw not in {_TRANSPORT_CLI, _TRANSPORT_SDK}:
84	        raise ValueError(
85	            f"{_TRANSPORT_ENV_VAR} must be {_TRANSPORT_CLI!r} or {_TRANSPORT_SDK!r}; got {raw!r}"
86	        )
87	    return raw
88	
89	
90	def _estimate_tokens(*, text: str) -> int:
91	    return max(1, len(text) // 4)
92	
93	
94	def _invoke_cli(*, prompt: str, model_id: str, max_tokens: int) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
95	    _ = max_tokens
96	    cmd: list[str] = [
97	        "claude",
98	        "-p",
99	        "-",
100	        "--model",
101	        model_id,
102	        "--output-format",
103	        "json",
104	    ]
105	    completed = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603 — local trusted CLI
106	        cmd,
107	        input=prompt,
108	        capture_output=True,
109	        text=True,
110	        timeout=_CLI_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
111	        check=False,
112	    )
113	    if completed.returncode != 0:
114	        stderr = (completed.stderr or "").strip()[:500]
115	        raise RuntimeError(f"claude CLI exited {completed.returncode}: {stderr or '(no stderr)'}")
116	    raw_stdout = (completed.stdout or "").strip()
117	    if len(raw_stdout) == 0:
118	        raise RuntimeError("claude CLI returned empty stdout")
119	    try:
120	        envelope = json.loads(raw_stdout)
121	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
122	        return (
123	            raw_stdout,
124	            _estimate_tokens(text=prompt),
125	            _estimate_tokens(text=raw_stdout),
126	        )
127	    if not isinstance(envelope, dict):
128	        return (
129	            raw_stdout,
130	            _estimate_tokens(text=prompt),
131	            _estimate_tokens(text=raw_stdout),
132	        )
133	    if envelope.get("is_error") is True:
134	        err = str(envelope.get("result", "")).strip()
135	        raise RuntimeError(f"claude CLI envelope is_error: {err[:300]}")
136	    text_obj = envelope.get("result", "")
137	    text = text_obj if isinstance(text_obj, str) else raw_stdout
138	    usage = envelope.get("usage", {})
139	    if isinstance(usage, dict):
140	        in_tok_obj = usage.get("input_tokens", _estimate_tokens(text=prompt))
141	        out_tok_obj = usage.get("output_tokens", _estimate_tokens(text=text))
142	        in_tok = (
143	            int(in_tok_obj)
144	            if isinstance(in_tok_obj, int | float)
145	            else _estimate_tokens(text=prompt)
146	        )
147	        out_tok = (
148	            int(out_tok_obj)
149	            if isinstance(out_tok_obj, int | float)
150	            else _estimate_tokens(text=text)
151	        )
152	    else:
153	        in_tok = _estimate_tokens(text=prompt)
154	        out_tok = _estimate_tokens(text=text)
155	    return text, in_tok, out_tok
156	
157	
158	def _invoke_sdk(*, prompt: str, model_id: str, max_tokens: int) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
159	    import anthropic  # noqa: PLC0415 — lazy import; only used when SDK transport is selected
160	
161	    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
162	    response = client.messages.create(
163	        model=model_id,
164	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
165	        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
166	    )
167	    text_parts: list[str] = []
168	    for block in response.content:
169	        block_type = getattr(block, "type", None)
170	        if block_type == "text":
171	            text_parts.append(getattr(block, "text", ""))
172	    text = "".join(text_parts)
173	    usage = response.usage
174	    in_tok = int(getattr(usage, "input_tokens", 0))
175	    out_tok = int(getattr(usage, "output_tokens", 0))
176	    return text, in_tok, out_tok
177	
178	
179	def _retryable_invoke(
180	    *,
181	    prompt: str,
182	    model_id: str,
183	    max_tokens: int,
184	    transport: str,
185	) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
186	    backoff = _INITIAL_BACKOFF_SECONDS
187	    last_exc: Exception | None = None
188	    for attempt in range(_MAX_RETRIES):
189	        try:
190	            if transport == _TRANSPORT_CLI:
191	                return _invoke_cli(prompt=prompt, model_id=model_id, max_tokens=max_tokens)
192	            return _invoke_sdk(prompt=prompt, model_id=model_id, max_tokens=max_tokens)
193	        except (RuntimeError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired, OSError) as exc:
194	            last_exc = exc
195	            if attempt + 1 >= _MAX_RETRIES:
196	                raise
197	            time.sleep(min(backoff, _MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS))
198	            backoff *= 2.0
199	        except Exception as exc:
200	            last_exc = exc
201	            transient = False
202	            try:
203	                import anthropic  # noqa: PLC0415
204	
205	                if isinstance(exc, anthropic.RateLimitError | anthropic.APIConnectionError):
206	                    transient = True
207	                if isinstance(exc, anthropic.APIStatusError) and exc.status_code in (
208	                    500,
209	                    502,
210	                    503,
211	                    504,
212	                    529,
213	                ):
214	                    transient = True
215	            except ImportError:
216	                pass
217	            if not transient or attempt + 1 >= _MAX_RETRIES:
218	                raise
219	            time.sleep(min(backoff, _MAX_BACKOFF_SECONDS))
220	            backoff *= 2.0
221	    if last_exc is not None:
222	        raise last_exc
223	    raise RuntimeError("retry loop terminated without raising")
224	
225	
226	def make_model_call(
227	    *,
228	    model_id: str,
229	    cost_tracker: CostTracker,
230	    max_tokens: int = 4096,
231	    secondary_cost_tracker: CostTracker | None = None,
232	) -> Callable[[str], str]:
233	    assert model_id != FORBIDDEN_HAIKU_MODEL_ID, (
234	        f"haiku model {FORBIDDEN_HAIKU_MODEL_ID!r} is forbidden by t0019 calibration finding"
235	    )
236	    transport = _selected_transport()
237

239	        if not isinstance(prompt, str):

241	        if len(prompt) == 0:
242	            raise ValueError("prompt must be non-empty")
243	        text, in_tok, out_tok = _retryable_invoke(
244	            prompt=prompt,
245	            model_id=model_id,
246	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
247	            transport=transport,
248	        )
249	        cost_tracker.record(
250	            model_id=model_id,
251	            prompt_tokens=in_tok,
252	            completion_tokens=out_tok,
253	        )
254	        if secondary_cost_tracker is not None:

256	                model_id=model_id,
257	                prompt_tokens=in_tok,
258	                completion_tokens=out_tok,
259	            )
260	        return text

content.content:
1	"""Minimal tool registry for the smoke harness.
2	
3	Provides a single ``python_exec`` tool that runs a snippet of Python in a subprocess sandbox with a
4	short timeout and small output cap. The same callable is wrapped two ways so the t0006 ReAct
5	delegate (which calls ``tool(**action.args)``) and the t0007 Plan-and-Solve delegate (which calls
6	``tool(args_str)``) can both invoke it without per-condition special-casing inside the harness.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import subprocess
12	from collections.abc import Callable, Mapping
13	from typing import Any, Final
14	
15	PYTHON_EXEC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: Final[float] = 5.0
16	PYTHON_EXEC_OUTPUT_CAP_BYTES: Final[int] = 64 * 1024
17	
18	
19	def python_exec_str(code: str) -> str:
20	    """Run ``code`` in a fresh ``python3`` subprocess; return stdout+stderr (truncated)."""
21	    if not isinstance(code, str):
22	        return f"<error: expected string, got {type(code).__name__}>"
23	    if len(code) == 0:
24	        return "<error: empty code>"
25	    try:
26	        completed = subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603 — local trusted invocation
27	            ["python3", "-c", code],
28	            capture_output=True,
29	            text=True,
30	            timeout=PYTHON_EXEC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
31	        )
32	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
33	        return f"<error: python_exec timed out after {PYTHON_EXEC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s>"
34	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — surface errors as observation
35	        return f"<error: python_exec invocation failed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}>"
36	    out: str = (completed.stdout or "") + (completed.stderr or "")
37	    if len(out) > PYTHON_EXEC_OUTPUT_CAP_BYTES:
38	        return out[:PYTHON_EXEC_OUTPUT_CAP_BYTES] + "\n<...truncated>"
39	    if len(out) == 0:
40	        return "<no output>"
41	    return out
42	
43	
44	def _react_python_exec(**kwargs: Any) -> str:
45	    """t0006-shape adapter: tool is called with ``code=<str>`` keyword arg."""
46	    code_obj: Any = kwargs.get("code", "")
47	    return python_exec_str(str(code_obj))
48	
49	
50	def _planandsolve_python_exec(args_str: str) -> str:
51	    """t0007/t0010-shape adapter: tool is called with the raw single-argument string.
52	
53	    The Plan-and-Solve / matched-mismatch executor passes the ``Action: <tool_name>(<args>)``
54	    payload as a single string. We treat the entire payload as the Python code to execute.
55	    """
56	    return python_exec_str(args_str)
57	
58	
59	def build_react_tool_registry() -> Mapping[str, Callable[..., Any]]:
60	    """Tool registry shape consumed by t0006 (kwargs)."""
61	    return {"python_exec": _react_python_exec}

2

4	plain Python files containing `TASKS = [Task(...), ...]` lists. We can download those
5	files directly without installing the package or providing API keys, parse the action
6	sequence length per task, and filter to tasks whose action count is in [4, 8]. If the
7	download fails, we keep the HumanEval pilot proxy and document the access attempt.

49	def _download_text(*, url: str, target: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:
50	    target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
51	    try:
52	        urllib.request.urlretrieve(url, target)
53	    except urllib.error.URLError as exc:
54	        return False, f"urllib.error.URLError fetching {url}: {exc}"
55	    except OSError as exc:
56	        return False, f"OSError fetching {url}: {exc}"
57	    if not target.exists() or target.stat().st_size == 0:
58	        return False, f"Empty file at {target} after fetching {url}"
59	    return True, "ok"

62	def _parse_action_counts(*, source_path: Path) -> list[int]:
63	    """Parse a tau-bench tasks Python file and return action counts per task.
64	
65	    The file is expected to contain `TASKS = [Task(annotator=..., user_id=...,
66	    instruction=..., actions=[Action(...), Action(...)], outputs=[...]), ...]`. We use
67	    the AST module to count the number of elements in each task's `actions` keyword arg.
68	    """
69	    src: str = source_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
70	    tree: ast.Module = ast.parse(src, filename=str(source_path))
71	    counts: list[int] = []
72	    for node in ast.walk(tree):
73	        if not isinstance(node, ast.Assign):
74	            continue
75	        targets = node.targets
76	        if len(targets) != 1:
77	            continue
78	        tgt = targets[0]
79	        if not isinstance(tgt, ast.Name) or not tgt.id.startswith("TASKS"):
80	            continue
81	        value = node.value
82	        if not isinstance(value, ast.List):
83	            continue
84	        for task_call in value.elts:
85	            if not isinstance(task_call, ast.Call):
86	                continue
87	            for kw in task_call.keywords:
88	                if kw.arg != "actions":
89	                    continue
90	                if isinstance(kw.value, ast.List):
91	                    counts.append(len(kw.value.elts))
92	                else:
93	                    counts.append(0)
94	    return counts
95	
96	
97	def _extract_task_records(
98	    *,
99	    source_path: Path,
100	    domain: str,
101	    split: str,
102	) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
103	    """Extract task instances as plain dicts: instruction, action count, action names."""
104	    src: str = source_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
105	    tree: ast.Module = ast.parse(src, filename=str(source_path))
106	    records: list[dict[str, object]] = []
107	    for node in ast.walk(tree):
108	        if not isinstance(node, ast.Assign):
109	            continue
110	        targets = node.targets
111	        if len(targets) != 1:
112	            continue
113	        tgt = targets[0]
114	        if not isinstance(tgt, ast.Name) or not tgt.id.startswith("TASKS"):
115	            continue
116	        value = node.value
117	        if not isinstance(value, ast.List):
118	            continue
119	        for idx, task_call in enumerate(value.elts):
120	            if not isinstance(task_call, ast.Call):
121	                continue
122	            rec: dict[str, object] = {
123	                "domain": domain,
124	                "split": split,
125	                "task_index": idx,
126	            }
127	            for kw in task_call.keywords:
128	                if kw.arg in ("annotator", "user_id"):
129	                    if isinstance(kw.value, ast.Constant):
130	                        rec[str(kw.arg)] = kw.value.value
131	                elif kw.arg == "instruction" and isinstance(kw.value, ast.Constant):
132	                    rec["instruction"] = kw.value.value
133	                elif kw.arg == "actions" and isinstance(kw.value, ast.List):
134	                    action_names: list[str] = []
135	                    for action_call in kw.value.elts:
136	                        if not isinstance(action_call, ast.Call):
137	                            continue
138	                        for ak in action_call.keywords:
139	                            if ak.arg == "name" and isinstance(ak.value, ast.Constant):
140	                                action_names.append(str(ak.value.value))
141	                    rec["actions"] = action_names
142	                    rec["action_count"] = len(action_names)
143	                elif kw.arg == "outputs" and isinstance(kw.value, ast.List):
144	                    outputs: list[object] = []
145	                    for elt in kw.value.elts:
146	                        if isinstance(elt, ast.Constant):
147	                            outputs.append(elt.value)
148	                    rec["outputs"] = outputs
149	            records.append(rec)
150	    return records
